introduction.html.title: Introduction
introduction.html.description:
Campaign Overview A death curse plaguing all of Toril emanates from the jungle island of Chult. Ancient ruins and hordes of undead fill the mostly unexplored and dangerous jungles. The mercantile city of Port Nyanzaru stands as a bastion of civilization on the northern edge of the jungle. The tone of the game is fantasy Indiana Jones / The Mummy. Overall the jungle and especially the tomb are very dangerous and unforgiving; though some encounters will be light and even slightly 'wacky' fun.
The jungles of Chult are largely unexplored and very dangerous, filled with dinosaurs, strange goblins, and a lot of undead. Wilderness survival is a key element of much of the game.
The peninsula's only city is Port Nyanzaru, a bustling port built on trade, filled with unique wonders, and run by powerful and enigmatic Merchant Princes. Character Creation: The Death Curse (in part) affects people who have been previously raised from the dead (They started wasting away). If you like, your character can have folks in their life who have been raised from the dead before the adventure starts; this might help motivate them to pursue the main external goal of the story (but that's not required!). The Death Curse started about 20 days before the start of the adventure and is widely talked about.
Characters can be from Chult, from the Sword Coast, or from anywhere. The current start idea is that you are all adventurer’s with a benefactor in Baldur’s Gate (I might change this based on your characters)
The game will probably begin in Baldur's Gate unless all / most of you decide your characters are from Chult.
Native Races from Chult are: Humans, Dwarves, Tabaxi and Aarakocra (Let me know if you want more details) The Campaign is supposed to be difficult, so be as Meta in your character creation as you want, feel free to work together to synergize characters and mechanics All Dnd 5.5e content published by WOTC is fair game (if it was reprinted for 2024, you have to use the new stuff 😉). Any 3rd party / Homebrew just run it by me. Characters start at level 2, and get the basic starting equipment or the gold equivalent. You may use point buy for your stats, or roll for your stats (4d6dl 6 times to create an array.) Mechanics to Keep in Mind: Revive magic doesn't work anymore. Traveling
On the map of Chult, each hex measures 10 miles across. Characters moving at a normal pace can travel 1 hex per day on foot through coastal, jungle, mountain, swamp, or wasteland terrain. They can travel 2 hexes per day if they're traveling by canoe on a river or lake. If characters move at a fast pace, they have a 50% chance to advance 1 additional hex that day. Characters moving at a fast pace take a penalty to their passive Wisdom (Perception) scores. If characters set a slow pace, they have a 50% chance to advance 1 fewer hex that day. Characters moving at a slow pace can move stealthily. As long as they're not in the open, they can try to surprise or sneak by other creatures they encounter. Dehydration At the end of each day, any character who hasn't drunk at least 2 gallons of fresh water must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or suffer 1 level of exhaustion. The saving throw is made with disadvantage if the character is wearing medium armor, heavy armor, or heavy clothing. Characters traveling at a fast pace, instead of a normal or slow pace, take a penalty on their saving throws against dehydration. A long rest will not restore exhaustion until the character consumes enough fresh water. Weather
Chult is hot, humid, and rainy throughout the year. The temperature regularly climbs as high as 35 degrees C during the day and seldom falls below 20 degrees C even at night. A day without rain is rare, but rain varies from a steady mist to drenching downpours. Visibility in heavy rain is limited to 50 yards. Beyond that distance, only Huge or larger objects can be distinguished. Missile weapon ranges are halved during rain.

locations/chult.html.title: Chult
locations/chult.html.description:
Location and Composition: Chult is a peninsula ringed with mountains and choked with rainforests. It is described as a tropical wilderness. The interior consists mostly of jungles, plateaus, impassable mountains, and belching volcanoes. Climate: The region is consistently hot, humid, and rainy throughout the year. A day without rain is rare Navigation Challenges: Mapping the peninsula has historically been nearly impossible. Rivers are often sluggish, making it difficult to discern upstream from downstream. Perils of the Wilds: The interior jungle is prowled by enormous reptiles (dinosaurs) and an army of undead. The only major settlement and safest entry point into Chult is Port Nyanzaru, located on the coast at the south end of the Bay of Chult.The party is aware of various local groups and pervasive dangers: Undead: The jungle is overrun by a blight of undead creatures. Chultans: The native human population is known as Chultans.
Other Races: Members of every race can be found in Port Nyanzaru.

headers.heading: Civilization: Port Nyanzaru

lore/soulmonger.html.title: Soulmonger
lore/soulmonger.html.description:
The Soulmonger is a necromantic artifact. It is the traced source of the worldwide "death curse".The function of the Soulmonger is directly tied to the two main aspects of the curse: Wasting Affliction: It is draining the life force from individuals who have been previously raised from the dead. For instance, the party's benefactor, Syndra Silvane. Preventing Resurrection: It is preventing future resurrections.
Intelligence Source: Information about the artifact was gathered by Syndra Silvane through her contacts, which included the Harpers. The Harpers determined the cause of the death curse is this device.
General Location: The Soulmonger is believed to be hidden on the peninsula of Chult.
Mission Goal: The characters have been charged with traveling to Chult to locate and neutralize the Soulmonger.

npcs/syndra-silvane.html.title: Syndra Silvane
npcs/syndra-silvane.html.description:
The characters know the following about Syndra Silvane: Role and Status: She is the party's benefactor, a retired adventurer, and a merchant. The Death Curse: Syndra revealed that she is afflicted by the worldwide "death curse" because she was raised from the dead years ago. This affliction causes her to wither and die, a process that conventional clerics cannot stop. Past Experience: She mentioned that she had died once and been raised from the dead, and has since closed the door on her adventuring life. She also confirmed that she had already sent other, more experienced parties on this quest when pressed by the adventurers Syndra Silvane laid out the following information regarding the expedition: The Source of the Curse: Her contacts, including the Harpers, determined that the death curse originates from a necromantic artifact called the Soulmonger.
The Objective: She tasked the adventurers with traveling to the peninsula of Chult to locate and neutralize the Soulmonger before the curse claims her life. Reward: She promised each character one rare and one uncommon magic item upon the successful completion of the quest.
Travel and Support: To expedite their journey, Syndra used a teleport spell to transport the group directly to Port Nyanzaru, the only major settlement in Chult. She provided the party with an incomplete map of Chult.
Post-Arrival Plans: Upon arrival, Syndra departed to stay with her friend, Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu. She recommended securing lodging at either the Thundering Lizard or Kaya's House of Repose. She informed the characters that Wakanga O'tamu is one of the seven merchant princes who rule the city.

sessions/session-1.html.title: Session 1
sessions/session-1.html.description:
The first session of the Tomb of Annihilation module began with the presentation of the worldwide "death curse". This affliction targets everyone who has ever been raised from the dead, causing them to wither and die, and preventing future resurrections.The player characters—including Mistral Honeygleam (a cheerful, small, level 1 caster), and Sir Caldryn Keyne (an arrogant, well-dressed, wealthy High Elf/Sun Elf paladin)—were gathered at a house in Baldur's Gate. They were acquainted and had worked together previously.
The party's benefactor, Syndra Silvane, revealed she was afflicted by the curse because she had been raised from the dead years ago. She consulted with contacts, including the Harpers, who traced the source of the curse to a necromantic artifact called the Soulmonger. The artifact is believed to be hidden within the ruins of an older civilization deep within Chult.
Syndra Silvane charged the adventurers with traveling to the peninsula of Chult to locate and neutralize the Soulmonger before the curse fully drained her life force. As a reward, she promised each character one rare and one uncommon magic item upon successful completion of the quest.
To expedite travel, Syndra Silvane intended to use a teleport spell to transport the party to the only major settlement in the region, Port Nyanzaru. The characters agreed, though Tidus Thalorien initially preferred to travel by sea.
Upon arrival, the party found themselves in the bustling, colorful, and noisy tropical city of Port Nyanzaru. Syndra Silvane departed to stay with her friend, Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu. She recommended the adventurers secure lodging at either the Thundering Lizard (for a "raucous time") or Kaya's House of Repose (for a "good night's sleep"), both located near the Red Bazaar.
Sir Caldryn Keyne resolved to seek lodging at the Thundering Lizard for revelry. Mistral Honeygleam spoke to the harbormaster (described as a large half gold dragon, half man) for directions to the Red Bazaar.While in the city, the party began investigating locations and seeking guidance:
Wakanga O'tamu: Sir Caldryn Keyne determined that the Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu dealt primarily in magic items and kept knowledge/information in scrolls.
Guide and Side Quest: While at the inn, a middle-aged woman approached the party, offering her services as a guide. She asked if they were heading toward Mbala. She revealed that she was seeking to eradicate the evil dwelling in the ghost town of Mbala, specifically the hag known as Nanny Pu'pu. The guide offered her services for free if the party agreed to help her destroy this evil. The party accepted this arrangement. The guide agreed to meet them at sunrise the following morning, confirming that she would handle guiding and navigation but the party was responsible for their food and water. The party purchased essential gear, including insect repellent balm and incense and rain catchers, and planned to start their expedition the next day.

did-you-level-up-last-session.html.title: DID YOU LEVEL UP LAST SESSION
did-you-level-up-last-session.html.sourcePath: DID YOU LEVEL UP LAST SESSION.md

locations/port-nyanzaru/port-nyanzaru.ht.title: Port Nyanzaru
locations/port-nyanzaru/port-nyanzaru.ht.description:
Atmosphere and Appearance: The city is bustling, colorful, and noisy. Buildings are painted in bright shades of blue, green, orange, and salmon pink.Governance: The city is ruled by seven Merchant Princes, who control monopolies on all merchandise sold within the city.Economy and Hazards: The city is surrounded by enemies, including ferocious reptiles and murderous undead. Dinosaurs are commonly used as beasts of burden to haul carts and tow boats along canals. Ekene-Afa Ifan Talro'a Jessamine Jobal (Guides and Sellswords) Kwayothé Wakanga O'tamu (Magic and knowledge) Zhanthi The Red Bazaar: The Red Bazaar is where locals shop for everyday needs such as meat, vegetables, fruit, insect repellent, and rain catchers.
The Thundering Lizard (Inn): It is known that wilderness guides frequent this establishment looking for work.
Kaya's House of Repose (Inn): It is known to be much quieter than the Thundering Lizard.
The Grand Souk: Traders from up and down the Sword Coast come to buy timber, spices, medicines, dinosaur parts, ivory, and other riches of Chult here. The Lighthouse: A flame burns atop this structure day and night to guide ships through darkness, fog, and rain into the harbor Fort Nyanzaru: This fort contains a winch for raising and lowering an immense iron chain stretched between the fort and the lighthouse. When the chain is raised, it prevents any ship from sailing into or out of the harbor. The fort also mounts two ballistas. The Warehouse District: This area consists of warehouses used to store goods being shipped into or out of Port Nyanzaru. Canals are cut between the rows of warehouses, allowing ships to be towed directly to the desired warehouse by harnessed dinosaurs for easy loading and unloading.

locations/port-nyanzaru/the-thundering-l.title: The Thundering Lizard
locations/port-nyanzaru/the-thundering-l.description:
The Thundering Lizard is a well-known establishment in Port Nyanzaru, recommended as a place of lodging for those seeking a lively atmosphere.It is characterized by its rowdy atmosphere. For adventurers seeking a stay focused on revelry,The Thundering Lizard played a role in the party's preparations before departing on their first expedition:
Sir Caldryn Keyne resolved to seek lodging at the Thundering Lizard specifically for revelry.
While staying at the inn, Sir Caldryn Keyne spent time there attempting to gather rumors from locals regarding the city's princes and the island of Chult.
This activity led to an encounter with two guards who were looking for Sir Caldryn Keyne.

locations/mbala.html.title: Mbala
locations/mbala.html.description:
Mbala is a significant location in the uncharted jungle of Chult, notable as the destination of the adventurers' first major expedition.
Mbala is the location of the first major objective undertaken by the party in the island of Chult.
The town of Mbala is the designated target for the party's current journey, initiated during their time in Port Nyanzaru.
A guide, a woman named Eku, approached the party seeking to hire them.
Eku revealed that she was seeking to eradicate the evil dwelling in the town of Mbala.
The specific source of this evil is known as Nanny Pu'pu. The guide offered her services for free if the party agreed to help her destroy this evil, an arrangement the adventurers accepted.
The party agreed to help defeat Nanny Pu'pu in the town of Mbala and are currently traveling toward this destination.

npcs/eku.html.title: Eku
npcs/eku.html.description:
Eku is a guide encountered in Port Nyanzaru who offered her services to the adventurers in exchange for assistance on her own quest.
Eku approached the party while they were at an inn in Port Nyanzaru, seeking to be hired.
Eku revealed she was embarking on a personal quest and inquired if the party was heading toward Mbala.
Her mission is to eradicate the evil dwelling in the town of Mbala, specifically targeting Nanny Pu'pu. Eku offered her guiding services for free if the party agreed to help her destroy this evil. The party accepted her offer and are currently traveling with her. Eku confirmed that she would handle guiding and navigation for the expedition, although the party remained responsible for their food and water.

headers.heading: Role in Port Nyanzaru

npcs/merchant-prince-jobal.html.title: Merchant Prince Jobal
npcs/merchant-prince-jobal.html.description:
Jobal is one of the seven influential figures who hold the title of Merchant Prince and collectively rule the city of Port Nyanzaru.Jobal's specific domain relates to procuring manpower. Jobal deals exclusively in guides and sellswords. Like the other Merchant Princes, Jobal conducts business and governance from Goldenthrone
Jobal was introduced to the party through the local authorities while Sir Caldryn Keyne was attempting to recruit companions in Port Nyanzaru:
Sir Caldryn Keyne was intercepted by city guards and informed that attempts to recruit an entourage were unauthorized. The guards explained that Merchant Prince Jobal handles guides and sellswords in the city.
Sir Caldryn Keyne was advised to seek an audience with Jobal to follow proper protocol for hiring staff.

headers.heading: Role and Authority in Port Nyanzaru

npcs/nanny-pu'pu.html.title: Nanny Pu'pu
npcs/nanny-pu'pu.html.description:
Nanny Pu'pu is an infamous figure dwelling in the jungle of Chult. She is the central figure of the first major quest undertaken by the adventurers.
She currently dwells in the town of Mbala.
The existence of Nanny Pu'pu became relevant to the party during initial inquiries in Port Nyanzaru while they were seeking guides and information about their expedition into Chult.
A woman named Eku approached the party at an inn and offered her services as a guide.
Eku revealed that she was seeking to eradicate an evil dwelling in Mbala, specifically naming Nanny Pu'pu.
Eku offered her services for free if the party agreed to help her destroy this evil.

pcs/mistral-honeygleam.html.title: Mistral Honeygleam
pcs/mistral-honeygleam.html.description:
Mistral Honeygleam is a small, cheerful Fairy known for her bright outlook and idealistic nature. A "Day Dreamer" by background, she views the world as a story waiting to unfold and is rarely seen without her most treasured possession: a collection of graphic romantic tales.Mistral often seeks quiet moments of reflection, such as during the party's stay in Port Nyanzaru in Session 2, where she spent her evening reading on the roof of the Thundering Lizard inn. Her idealism sometimes leads her to refuse to see the world for its harsher realities, preferring to focus on how she can make it brighter through her dreams and magic. This personality trait is reflected in her interactions with the group, where she maintains a helpful and optimistic presenceDespite her small stature, Mistral has proven to be an essential member of the expedition due to her magical utility and keen senses.
Provisioning: Upon entering the Chultan wilderness in Session 2, she utilized her Goodberry magic to ensure the party remained well-provisioned for the arduous trek. Environmental Awareness: She frequently uses Druidcraft to predict the local weather, accurately identifying a day-long drizzle during their first day of travel.
Scouting and Investigation: Her ability to fly allows her to reach areas others cannot. In Session 3, she investigated the remains of a fallen explorer suspended forty feet in the jungle canopy, retrieving a note that explained his tragic fate. Mistral is accompanied by a flying monkey familiar that has become a distinct character within the group, often acting on its own mischievous or heroic impulses.
Life-Saving Intervention: During a violent dinosaur ambush in Session 2, the monkey saved the life of Sir Caldryn Keyne by surreptitiously feeding him a Goodberry after he was incapacitated.
Levity and Mischief: In Session 3, while the party was performing a somber burial for a fallen elf, the familiar provided a moment of levity by swapping the gold funeral coins Caldryn placed on the body for copper pieces.
In battle, Mistral blends her fey nature with tactical magic and martial skill. She often utilizes her flight to stay out of reach of land-bound predators while supporting her allies. During a fierce encounter with a pack of Allosauruses in Session 3, she demonstrated her defensive capabilities by using magic—likely her Shield spell—to protect herself from claws before counterattacking with her scimitar. She was also a key participant in the tactical defense against goblin scavengers following the collapse of the House of the Man and Crocodile in Session 6.

pcs/scarloc-farmguard.html.title: Scarloc Farmguard
pcs/scarloc-farmguard.html.description:
Scarloc Farmguard is a Wood Elf fighter and former farmer known for his physical discipline and survival instincts. Despite his humble origins, he has become a core martial presence and a primary navigator for the expedition into Chult.
Scarloc’s background as a farmer contributes to his keen sense of the land, though he often bolsters this with his Tactical Mind ability to maintain his bearings. In Session 2, he successfully charted a course through difficult terrain, allowing the party to cover two hexes in a single day. While he generally keeps the group on track, such as in Session 3 when he navigated another two hexes of dense jungle, he is not infallible. During the torrential rainstorms of Session 4, despite his tactical focus, he eventually realized the party had circled back to a previous location after becoming lost in the dense canopy. Beyond direction-finding, Scarloc has demonstrated a talent for tracking, notably identifying the footprints of a massive creature in Session 4 which allowed the party to avoid a potentially deadly encounter.
In combat, Scarloc is a powerhouse who favors the greatsword, often utilizing his Graze mastery to ensure he deals damage even when a strike doesn't land cleanly. He has a unique relationship with Sir Caldryn Keyne, who has taken him on as a "protege" for lessons in being a "chivalrous hero". This tutelage continued into Session 3, where Caldryn offered combat tips even while fumbling with his own gear. Scarloc has repeatedly proven his worth in the field, from landing decisive blows against raptors in Session 2 to striking heavy blows against a pack of Allosauruses in Session 3. In Session 6, he was instrumental in the tactical victory over a band of goblin scavengers who ambushed the party’s camp.
Scarloc maintains a rigorous physical regimen, often performing aerobic exercises even during long rests in civilization. This fitness is vital for the grueling work of setting up fortified camps in the jungle, a task he frequently performs for the group. His journey has not been without injury; in Session 5, while exploring the House of the Man and Crocodile, he accidentally triggered a scything blade trap that dealt him significant injury. He also narrowly escaped the collapse of the shrine's final chamber in Session 6, surviving the rain of heavy stones to help recover the party's prize.

pcs/sir-caldryn-keyne.html.title: Sir Caldryn Keyne
pcs/sir-caldryn-keyne.html.description:
Sir Caldryn Keyne is a wealthy elf from the Upper City of Baldur’s Gate, characterized by an impeccable, polished demeanor that often borders on arrogant. Raised in a world of "velvet privilege".High-Born Disposition and Social ManeuversCaldryn often carries the habits of high society into the wilderness, such as serving wine from his private reserve and attempting to recruit a personal entourage even in Port Nyanzaru. His high charisma allows him to navigate social friction with ease; for example, in Session 2, he successfully defused a tense encounter with city guards by claiming his unauthorized recruitment attempts were merely a search for a "young intern for tutelage". He has also demonstrated a cunning use of his magical nature to bypass obstacles, such as in Session 3, when he "tricked" a Flaming Fist patrol by surrendering his magical pact weapon as collateral for an exploration charter, knowing he could simply re-summon it later.Tutelage and Chivalric Ideals
Caldryn views himself as a mentor, frequently engaging in the "tutelage" of Scarloc Farmguard on the finer points of being a "chivalrous hero". Despite this self-appointed role as a paragon of valor, his early experiences in the jungle highlighted a frivolous nature that occasionally put the group at risk. In Session 3, following an ambush where he was nearly killed by dinosaurs, he displayed a rare moment of humility by kneeling in the mud to apologize to Mistral and vowing to be better prepared. Even when fumbling with his own gear, he maintained his focus on noble ritual, such as delivering a short eulogy and placing gold coins on the eyes of a fallen elf explorer to "pay the ferryman".Divine Support and Crisis of Faith
In combat, Caldryn blends martial prowess with divine protection, often bolstering his allies with Lay on Hands, Shield of Faith, and Armor of Agathys. While he initially presented himself as a disciple of the god Tyr, his internal life is more complex. During a somber moment at a riverbank in Session 6, Caldryn made a startling confession to his companions, revealing a profound crisis of faith. He admitted that his divine powers do not actually originate from Tyr, but instead stem from his own inner self-belief.Resourcefulness and Exploration
Caldryn’s keen eye for magical threats was instrumental during the party's trek through the jungle. In Session 4, he used magic to detect a glyph of warding trap on a statue of Ubtao. His willingness to participate in the "rider and carrier" protocol of the shrine demonstrated his commitment to the group's success, eventually leading to the recovery of the Alchemy Jug in Session 6.

pcs/tidus-thalorien.html.title: Tidus Thalorien
pcs/tidus-thalorien.html.description:
Tidus Thalorien is a High Elf Druid and Sage whose connection to nature is often expressed through his reverence for water and his pragmatic support of the expedition. While he possesses powerful offensive magic, he frequently supports the group ensuring the party’s basic survival needs—such as water and direction—are met through divine intervention.Tidus maintains a deep devotion to the water goddess, a trait first seen during his preference for sea travel over magical teleportation during the party's departure for Chult in Session 1. This piety is a daily practice; before the expedition officially entered the jungle in Session 2, he performed a ritual of prayer to give thanks for the journey ahead. His elemental affinity serves a practical purpose in the harsh jungle environment; for instance, in Session 3, he utilized his magic to magically refill the party's water containers, preventing exhaustion and dehydration during their trek. Resource Management: Beyond securing water, Tidus is an active participant in camp life, helping to secure the perimeter and prepare food during the group’s first night in the wilds.
Compassion for Wildlife: Tidus has demonstrated a unique ability to communicate with the creatures of Chult. In Session 5, upon discovering a panicked young axe beak trapped in a pen at Camp Righteous, he used magic to speak with the bird and ultimately convinced the party to release it back into the wild. Tidus is a formidable combatant who uses a mix of frost magic and decisive strikes.
Precision Strikes: In Session 3, he provided critical support against a pack of Allosauruses by chilling the predators with Ray of Frost. He later demonstrated his lethality in Session 4 when he delivered the final blow to an ambushing tiger using a True Strike.
Area Defense: During the chaotic goblin ambush in Session 6, Tidus transitioned to powerful area-of-effect magic, utilizing spells like Thunderwave to drive back scavengers and protect the weary party following their escape from the collapsing House of the Man and Crocodile.
Tidus has survived some of the expedition's most harrowing trials. In Session 5 and Session 6, he helped navigate the trap-riddled shrine of the Man and Crocodile. He was present during the Great Collapse of the shrine's treasury, narrowly escaping the falling debris before working with his companions to excavate their prize: the Alchemy Jug.

sessions/session-2.html.title: Session 2
sessions/session-2.html.description:
The adventurers had previously secured essential gear, such as insect repellent balm and rain catchers. Sir Caldryn Keyne spent time at the Thundering Lizard inn attempting to gather rumors from locals about the city's princes and the island. This led to an encounter with two guards who were looking for him.
The guards informed Sir Caldryn Keyne that his attempts to recruit companions for his entourage were unauthorized, noting that Merchant Prince Jobal handles guides and sellswords in the city. Sir Caldryn Keyne defused the situation by claiming he was merely seeking a young intern for tutelage, and he was advised to seek an audience with Jobal to follow proper protocol.
The party settled in for their long rest. Scarloc spent his time doing aerobic exercises and Mistral reading on the roof of the inn.
The next morning, preparations focused on the journey ahead. The party confirmed they would be traveling with the guide they met previously, who offered her services for free in exchange for help defeating Nanny Pu'pu in the town of Mbala.
During breakfast, the Druid, Mistral, used their daily ability to produce Goodberry to help provision the group for the journey. Tidus performed a ritual of prayer, giving thanks to the water goddess.After meeting their guide, the party decided to travel at a fast pace to make rapid progress, driven by the urgency of the death curse. A Druidcraft cantrip confirmed the weather would be a constant drizzle for the entire day.
With the aid of Tidus Thalorien's Guidance cantrip and the guide (Eku) the navigator (Scarloc) successfully charted a course, allowing the party to cover two hexes of the jungle during their first day of travel. The terrain was difficult, described as messy and mushy, though the guide suggested a route that bypassed areas of known undead activity.
As night fell, the party set up camp. Mistral Honeygleam, Tidus Thalorien, and Scarloc Farmguard worked together to secure the camp, setting up their tents and preparing food using the supplies, supplemented by the magical berries. During this time, Sir Caldryn Keyne continued his efforts at "tutelage" of Scarloc, sharing lessons on being a "chivalent hero" and serving wine from his private reserve.The long rest was violently interrupted when the camp was ambushed by large, raptor-like dinosaurs.
In the ensuing combat, Sir Caldryn Keyne was immediately incapacitated by a powerful strike. His life was saved by Mistral's flying monkey familiar, which provided him with a Goodberry, returning him to 1 HP. The rest of the party engaged the monsters. Scarloc Farmguard landed decisive blows. The party ultimately defeated the three dinosaurs.

factions/flaming-fist.html.title: Flaming Fist
factions/flaming-fist.html.description:
The Flaming Fist is a powerful mercenary company based in the city of Baldur's Gate that currently maintains a significant military presence on the peninsula of Chult. They operate as a disciplined faction claiming jurisdiction over the region's wilderness and its potential treasures.Presence and Authority
The organization’s primary base of operations in Chult is Fort Beluarian, a wooden stockade located on a hillock north of Port Nyanzaru. From this stronghold, they dispatch patrols into the jungle to "pacify" the territory.Exploration ChartersThe Flaming Fist enforces strict legal control over the Chultan interior, requiring all explorers to possess an Exploration Charter.
• Requirement: It is considered illegal to explore or plunder Chult without this official paperwork.• Cost: Based on the party's recent encounter, a charter costs 50 gold pieces (60 for a field charter).• Enforcement: Fist patrols are authorized to treat the gear of unauthorized explorers as "contraband" and may resort to attacking expeditions and confiscating their equipment if a charter cannot be produced.
During their third night in the jungle, the party was intercepted by a Flaming Fist patrol of a dozen soldiers carrying torches. The patrol's captain demanded to see the group's charter and threatened to seize their equipment as contraband. Through tense diplomacy and high charisma, Sir Caldryn Keyne negotiated a deal to secure a charter for the group.
The party paid 20 gold pieces upfront and provided Sir Caldryn's ornate Warhammer as collateral for the remaining 40 gold balance. Unknown to the patrol, the weapon was a magical pact weapon that Caldryn could secretly re-summon later, effectively allowing the party to continue their journey toward Mbala with the necessary legal protections.

sessions/session-3.html.title: Session 3
sessions/session-3.html.description:
The third session of the expedition follows the party—Mistral Honeygleam, Sir Caldryn Keyne, Scarloc Farmguard, and Tidus Thalorien—as they recover from their first night in the jungle and face the complexities of Chultan law and environmental hazards.
Following the dinosaur ambush that nearly claimed his life, Sir Caldryn Keyne experiences a rare moment of humility. Usually arrogant and polished, he wakes to find himself covered in mud and fumbling with his gear, struggling to pack his tent as he is completely out of his element. He approaches Mistral Honeygleam and, kneeling in the mud, vows to be better prepared and thanks her for saving his life, acknowledging that his frivolousness previously put the group in danger. Despite this shift, he maintains his "protege" relationship with Scarloc, offering tips on valiant combat even as he fumbles with his own equipment.
The party confirms they will continue traveling at a fast pace, hoping to bypass known regions of undead activity. With the guide Eku leading and Scarloc navigating, the group successfully covers two more hexes of dense jungle.
During their journey, they make a somber discovery: the body of an elf hanging from a branch forty feet above the ground. Mistral flies up to investigate and finds a note explaining that the unfortunate explorer had climbed the tree to escape dinosaurs but was too terrified to descend, eventually perishing from his predicament. In a show of respect, the party decides to bury the remains. Caldryn delivers a short eulogy and places two gold coins on the elf's eyes to "pay the ferryman". However, in a moment of levity, Mistral’s flying monkey surreptitiously swaps the gold coins for copper once the body is in the grave.Their progress was soon challenged when they were ambushed by a pack of three Allosauruses that emerged from the dense undergrowth.
In the ensuing struggle, Scarloc Farmguard landed heavy blows with his greatsword, and Sir Caldryn Keyne utilized his Divine Smite and Warhammer to drive the predators back. Mistral Honeygleam used her magic to shield herself from the dinosaurs' claws before counterattacking with her scimitar, while Tidus Thalorien provided support by chilling the beasts with Ray of Frost. After the party successfully dispatched two of the predators, the final bloodied dinosaur fled into the woods, allowing the group to resume their trek
As the party settles in for their third night in the jungle, Tidus uses magic to refill their water containers, ensuring they remain hydrated for the coming day. Their rest is interrupted not by monsters, but by a patrol of a dozen torches. The group is intercepted by the Flaming Fist, a mercenary faction that claims jurisdiction over the region.
The patrol's captain demands to see the party's Exploration Charter, noting that it is illegal to explore or plunder Chult without one. Facing the threat of having their gear confiscated as "contraband," the party engages in tense diplomacy. Caldryn uses his high charisma to influence the soldiers, eventually negotiating a deal:• The party pays 20 gold upfront toward the 60 gold cost of a charter.
• As collateral for the remaining balance, Caldryn surrenders his ornate Warhammer to the captain.
• Crucially, Caldryn’s weapon is a magical pact weapon, meaning he can simply re-summon it later, effectively "tricking" the patrol into accepting a temporary asset as permanent collateral.
Satisfied for the moment, the Flaming Fist issues the party a charter and allows them to continue their journey toward Mbala.

headers.heading: Continued Expedition and a Grim Discovery
Encounter with the Flaming Fist

lore/ubtao.html.title: Ubtao
lore/ubtao.html.description:
Ubtao is a god who served as the primary protector of Chult and its civilization for centuries. He is widely known by the title "Great Father".
According to the guide Eku, Ubtao once guided the people of Chult but eventually abandoned them. It is believed he grew angry at his worshipers' endless warring and their constant reliance on him to solve every problem, leading him to leave the land entirely.
The party discovered a ten-foot-tall statue of a Chultan King overgrown with vines, which Eku identified as a representation of Ubtao. (Session 4)

headers.heading: Abandonment of Chult

sessions/session-4.html.title: Session 4
sessions/session-4.html.description:
The fourth session of the expedition saw the party—Mistral Honeygleam, Sir Caldryn Keyne, Scarloc Farmguard, and Tidus Thalorien—grappling with the legalities of their journey, the harsh Chultan climate, and a series of dangerous encounters while navigating toward their objectives.
Following the previous encounter with the Flaming Fist, Sir Caldryn Keyne questioned their guide, Eku, regarding the legitimacy of their expedition and why she did not already possess a charter. Eku clarified that the Flaming Fist are "illegitimate thugs" not sanctioned by the Merchant Princes of Port Nyanzaru. She explained that obtaining a charter would have required a dangerous trip to Fort Beluarian, which most guides avoid. Despite the tension, the party resolved to continue under her guidance.The journey through the jungle was hampered by erratic weather and difficult terrain:
Environmental Hazards: The group faced alternating days of torrential rainstorms and stifling humidity. Scarloc Farmguard utilized his Tactical Mind to maintain his bearings during the heavy storms.
Getting Lost: The party attempted a fast pace but eventually became lost in the dense canopy. After a day of travel, Scarloc realized they had circled back near a previous location.
Camp Righteous: Realizing their proximity to the riverbank, the party discussed visiting Camp Righteous. Eku noted the camp is rumored to be abandoned and sits on the edge of undead territory. The party decided to investigate the camp to potentially secure supplies. The party's journey was interrupted by various denizens of the rainforest:
Statue of Ubtao: The group discovered a ten-foot-tall statue of a Chultan God overgrown with vines. Eku identified it as a representation of Ubtao, the "Great Father".
Magical Traps and Tracks: Sir Caldryn Keyne used magic to detect a glyph of warding placed on the statue as a trap. Nearby, Scarloc identified extremely large tracks belonging to a massive creature, which the party chose to avoid.
The Tiger Ambush: While traveling toward the river, the party was ambushed by a tiger. The group successfully dispatched the predator, with Tidus Thalorien delivering the final blow using a True Strike. Girallons and Stegosaurus: The party spotted a group of four-armed apes (Girallons) sleeping in the trees and successfully used stealth to bypass them. Later, a massive stegosaurus wandered through their camp; finding it to be a peaceful herbivore, the party observed it without conflict. Myconid Sighting: During one night, a small group of myconids riding "mushroom dogs" entered the camp but fled immediately upon seeing the party was well-defended. As the party neared the riverbank in a heavy downpour, they were ambushed by six giant spiders.
Combat Highlights: The spiders utilized webs to restrain the party, and Scarloc Farmguard was briefly knocked unconscious after being overwhelmed.
Arcane and Divine Support: Sir Caldryn Keyne provided critical healing through Lay on Hands and bolstered defenses with Shield of Faith and Armor of Agathys. Mistral Honeygleam and her familiar distracted the creatures, allowing her to land decisive blows with her magic-imbued club.
Victory: Through coordinated efforts and Tidus Thalorien’s thalassic magic, the party eventually killed all six spiders, clearing the way to continue toward Camp Righteous.

headers.heading: Navigation Challenges and a New Destination

factions/order-of-the-gauntlet.html.title: Order of the Gauntlet
factions/order-of-the-gauntlet.html.description:
The Order of the Gauntlet is a faction of faithful monster-hunters and warriors dedicated to protecting others from the depredations of evildoers. In Chult, they have committed considerable resources to a primary mission: quelling the undead menace that plagues the jungle interior.
• Camp Righteous: This was the Order's riverside base camp, established around an ancient Chultan shrine known as the House of the Crocodile. Approximately two months ago, the camp was overrun by undead and the site was abandoned to the jungle.
• Camp Vengeance: Following the disaster at Camp Righteous, survivors retreated further upriver to the more heavily fortified base.
• Kotumur : A Dragonborn member of the Order encountered by the party at the ruins of Camp Righteous. He returned to the site of the disaster to assess the ruins and cautioned the adventurers about the deadly traps within the House of the Crocodile, which the Order had previously failed to bypass.
In Session 5, the party first encountered the Order through Kotumur while investigating the wreckage of Camp Righteous. Through him, the party learned of the Order's retreat to Camp Vengeance and received a warning that the Order had abandoned their own attempts to explore the House of the Crocodile due to the lethality of its magical and mechanical defenses.

locations/camp-righteous.html.title: Camp Righteous
locations/camp-righteous.html.description:
Camp Righteous is an abandoned riverside outpost in the Chultan jungle that formerly served as a forward base for the Order of the Gauntlet. It is located on the edge of territory infested with undead.
The camp was established by the Order of the Gauntlet. Approximately two months prior to the adventurers' arrival, the outpost was overrun by undead. The few survivors of the slaughter retreated further upriver toward Camp Vengeance.Upon discovery by the party, the camp was found in a state of total wreckage, filled with moldy, tattered tents and permanent structures that had been burned to the ground. Notable features discovered at the site include: The Great Statue: An 80-foot-tall stone statue carved to look like a man carrying a crocodile on his back dominates the northern ridge of the camp. Animal Pens: A crude enclosure was found containing a young, panicked axe beak that had survived on worms since the camp's fall; it was subsequently released by the party. Command and Soldiers' Tents: The remaining canvas structures are in various states of decay and rot. The House of the Crocodile is an ancient Chultan shrine built into the stony hillside behind the massive statue at Camp Righteous. Its entrance is a stone archway located directly between the feet of the Great Statue.
The shrine is notorious for its deadly magical and mechanical defenses, which the Order of the Gauntlet had abandoned their attempts to explore due to the lethality of the traps. Known hazards include: Concealed Pit Trap: A pit hidden under mud and debris near the entrance. Scything Blade Trap: Concealed wall niches that trigger blades to sweep across the corridor. The Puzzle Floor: A terrace featuring a four-by-four grid of floor tiles. Stepping on the incorrect sequence triggers magical flames that blast from the walls. The Puzzle Door: A wooden door that requires a specific sequence of tiles to be touched in order to open.
The innermost sanctum of the shrine, explored in Session 6, was a dusty, 40-foot-high vaulted chamber. The Central Pillar: At the heart of the room stood a 30-foot-tall stone pillar featuring a narrow, dusty spiral staircase of 30 steps. The pillar was decorated with reliefs depicting the Man and the Crocodile. Lightning Glyphs: The ascent was protected by magical Evocation glyphs on steps 3, 10, 17, and 23. These traps triggered 20-foot-radius spheres of lightning. The Alchemy Jug: Resting at the peak of the pillar was the shrine's prize: a large, beautiful ceramic Alchemy Jug.
The Great Collapse: Upon claiming the artifact in Session 6, the shrine's final defense was activated. The ceiling began to collapse, raining heavy stones and bricks down on the party and burying the chamber in rubble. The imagery of the Great Statue and the solution to the shrine's traps are based on a classic Chultan fable. In the story, a Crocodile tricked a Man into carrying him on his back across the entire realm of humans.

lore/fable-of-the-man-and-crocodile.html.title: Fable of the Man and Crocodile
lore/fable-of-the-man-and-crocodile.html.description: In the early days of the world, Man stood by the banks of a river, frightened. Crocodile raised his head from the water and asked, "What troubles you, cousin Man?"Man said, "I must cross this river, but I fear to enter the water alone, because it teems with your brethren."Crocodile replied, "It's true, you would not be safe. But I will carry you across the river safely on my back, if you promise to return the favor." Man agreed, and Crocodile bore him safely across the waterWhen they reached the far bank, Man asked, "How can I repay you?" Crocodile replied, "I wish to see the realm of Humans, but I fear to go there alone, because it teems with your brethren. You must carry me on your back across your realm." Man had been tricked, but a promise is a promise, so he carried Crocodile safely on his back across the entire realm of humans, a journey that lasted many years. He also swore, in his anger, that never again would Men and Crocodiles be friends, and so it has remained to this day.
lore/fable-of-the-man-and-crocodile.html.sourcePath: Lore/Fable of the Man and Crocodile.md

pcs/kotumur.html.title: Kotumur
pcs/kotumur.html.description:
Kotumur is a Dragonborn warrior and a dedicated member of the Order of the Gauntlet. He was encountered by the adventurers during their investigation of the ruins of Camp Righteous.
Kotumur was part of the garrison stationed at Camp Righteous before it was overrun by undead approximately two months prior to the party's arrival in Session 5. He returned to the abandoned site alone to assess the state of the ruins.Upon meeting the party, Kotumur provided several vital pieces of information regarding the region and the Order's status:
• The Retreat: He confirmed that the survivors of the disaster at Camp Righteous fled further upriver to Camp Vengeance.
• The Shrine's Dangers: He issued a stern warning regarding the House of the Crocodile, the ancient shrine built into the massive statue at the camp. He revealed that the Order of the Gauntlet had previously attempted to explore the shrine but abandoned their efforts due to its exceptionally deadly traps.

sessions/session-5.html.title: Session 5
sessions/session-5.html.description:
The fifth session follows the expedition—Mistral Honeygleam, Sir Caldryn Keyne, Scarloc Farmguard, and Tidus Thalorien—as they reach the abandoned ruins of Camp Righteous and attempt to navigate a trap-riddled shrine.
After a tiring day of travel through heavy downpours, the party arrived at Camp Righteous, an abandoned riverside outpost formerly held by the Order of the Gauntlet. The camp was a scene of wreckage, filled with moldy, tattered tents and burned permanent structures. Dominating the site was an 80-foot-tall stone statue carved to look like a man with a crocodile on his back.
Upon exploring the perimeter, the party encountered a Dragonborn named Kotumur, a member of the Order of the Gauntlet who had returned to assess the site. Kotumur confirmed that undead had overrun the camp two months prior, driving the survivors to retreat to Camp Vengeance further upriver.
During a search of the camp, the party discovered a young, panicked axe beak trapped in a crude animal pen. Using magic to speak with the bird, they learned it had survived on worms and scraps since the camp's fall. After providing it with rations, the party released the creature into the wild. The group then settled in for a long rest, with Scarloc Farmguard setting up the tents for the night.
The following morning, the adventurers turned their attention to the stone archway between the feet of the massive statue, which led to a dark tunnel. Kotumur cautioned that the Order of the Gauntlet had previously abandoned their attempts to explore the shrine due to its deadly traps.• Concealed Pit Trap: Near the entrance, the group identified a pit trap hidden under mud and debris. They eventually bypassed this by dragging one of the camp’s rowboats into the tunnel to serve as a makeshift bridge.
• Scything Blade Trap: Scarloc Farmguard triggered a trap that caused blades to sweep out from concealed wall niches, dealing him significant injury. The party jammed the mechanisms using metal tent spikes recovered from the camp.• The Puzzle Floor: Further inside, the party encountered a terrace with a tiled floor in a four-by-four pattern. Stepping on the incorrect tiles triggered magical flames that blasted from the walls, causing further damage to several party members.
Frustrated by the repeated traps, the party consulted Eku regarding the statue’s imagery. She recounted a classic Chultan fable in which a Crocodile tricked a Man into carrying him on his back across the entire realm of humans.The party realized the legend served as a set of instructions: When they touched the door's tiles in the correct sequence while one character carried another, the door swung open to reveal the treasuryThe final chamber was a dusty, 40-foot-high room littered with dinosaur bones. At the center stood a 30-foot-tall stone pillar with a narrow spiral staircase. At the top of the pillar sat the shrine's prize: a large, ceramic jug. Using magic, the party detected auras of conjuration and abjuration lingering around the pillar and the jug. The session ended as the party prepared to claim the artifact and depart the ruins.

headers.heading: Arrival at Camp Righteous
Entering the House of the Crocodile
The party’s initial exploration was fraught with danger:
The Fable of the Man and Crocodile

sessions/session-6.html.title: Session 6
sessions/session-6.html.description:
The Final Chamber: The adventurers reach the innermost sanctum of the massive statue, a vaulted chamber dominated by a 30-foot-tall central pillar. At the peak of the pillar sits a beautiful ceramic jug—the goal of their expedition. A Fragile Ascent: The party navigates a narrow, dusty staircase spiraling around the pillar, which is protected by magical Evocation glyphs that trigger when the correct protocol is not followed. The Great Collapse: Upon claiming the artifact, the shrine’s final defense is activated. The ceiling begins to collapse, raining heavy stones and bricks down on the party. A Desperate Escape: The group is forced into a frantic retreat as the chamber is destroyed. The escape is fraught with danger, as magical lightning safeguards and falling debris nearly claim the lives of several adventurers, leaving many unconscious. Recovering the Spoils: After the dust settles, the weary party uses Detect Magic to locate the prize buried beneath the rubble. Through collective effort, they excavate the Alchemy Jug, a potent magical item capable of producing various liquids. The Forest Ambush: Upon emerging from the ruins to take a much-needed rest, the group discovers their guide, Eku, has been knocked out in the undergrowth. They are immediately ambushed by a band of Goblins and a Goblin Boss who have surrounded the statue to scavenge its treasures. Tactical Victory: Despite their injuries, the party coordinates a successful defense, utilizing powerful area-of-effect spells like Thunderwave and Entangle to drive back the scavengers and secure their campsite.
A Secret Shared: During their rest at the riverbank, the evening takes a somber turn when Sir Caldryn Keyne makes a startling confession. He reveals that he is experiencing a crisis of faith, admitting that his divine powers do not come from the god Tyr, but rather from his own inner self-belief.
Moving Forward: The session concludes with the party recovering from their trials and preparing to continue their journey toward Mbala, having survived one of their most harrowing trials in Chult thus far.

sessions/session-7.html.title: Session 7
sessions/session-7.html.description:
Following the harrowing collapse of the House of the Crocodile, the adventurers began their journey away from the ruins of Camp Righteous, trekking through a persistent light drizzle toward their next destination, Camp Vengeance.Morning Reflections and New Gifts
The day began with the party processing Sir Caldryn Keyne’s recent confession regarding his faith. Despite the somber mood, Caldryn showed a rare gesture of kindness by gifting Mistral Honeygleam a "flowery fan" that he had carried from his private collection. Mistral provided breakfast for the group, and after some initial hesitation, they partook in the meal before setting off at a steady, normal pace through the dense undergrowth.Aerial Threats and Macabre Discoveries
As the expedition pushed deeper into the jungle, Scarloc Farmguard’s keen ears picked up the sound of erratic flapping from a nearby hollow tree. The party encountered a hostile swarm of bats that seemed unusually aggressive. After a brief but intense skirmish, the group investigated the area and made a grim discovery: the bats had been scavenging and carrying human remains. Among the debris, the party recovered a small cache of jewelry, suggesting the bats had preyed upon previous explorers.An Ambush in the CanopyLater in the day, while searching for a suitable place to rest, the party was suddenly ambushed by a group of ape-like predators. These creatures attacked from the heights of the trees, utilizing psychic abilities to disorient and stun several party members. The adventurers were forced into a difficult vertical battle, eventually driving the creatures back and slaying several of them. One of the predators managed to escape into a nearby nesting area high in the canopy.Settling InThe session concluded with the weary party securing a new campsite further away from the predators' territory. During a period of rest, they identified several items recovered from the day’s trials, including an Animal Friendship potion and a Alarm spell scroll, which they hoped would aid them in the trek ahead

did-you-level-up-last-session.html.sourcePath: DID YOU LEVEL UP LAST SESSION.md

lore/fable-of-the-man-and-crocodile.html.sourcePath: Lore/Fable of the Man and Crocodile.md

info_showCustomIcons.description: Show custom icons for files and folders

info_showDefaultFolderIcons.description: Show a default icon of a folder for every folder in the tree

info_showDefaultFileIcons.description: Show a default icon of a file for every file in the tree

info_defaultFolderIcon.description: The icon to use for folders. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_exposeStartingPath.description: Whether or not to show the current file in the file tree when the page is first loaded

info_minCollapseDepth.description: Only allow outline items to be collapsed if they are at least this many levels deep in the tree.

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site
